mission.en: an important task or duty that someone is given to do

ex.en: Her mission was to improve safety at the factory.
ex.ko: 그녀의 임무는 공장의 안전을 개선하는 것이었다.

mission.en: a group of people sent somewhere for a special purpose, such as diplomacy or aid

ex.en: The UN sent a peace mission to the region.
ex.ko: 유엔은 그 지역에 평화 사절단을 보냈다.

misty.en: covered with mist so that things are hard to see clearly

ex.en: The hills looked misty in the early morning.
ex.ko: 이른 아침 언덕은 안개가 껴 흐릿해 보였다.

misunderstanding.en: a situation in which people do not understand each other correctly

ex.en: There was a misunderstanding about the meeting time.
ex.ko: 회의 시간에 대해 오해가 있었다.

mixing bowl.en: a large bowl used for mixing ingredients together, especially when cooking

ex.en: She poured the flour into a mixing bowl.
ex.ko: 그녀는 밀가루를 반죽 그릇에 부었다.

modal verb.en: a verb such as 'can' or 'will' that is used with another verb to express ability, permission, or possibility

ex.en: 'Can' and 'must' are common modal verbs in English.
ex.ko: 'can'과 'must'는 영어에서 흔한 조동사이다.

modem.en: a device that connects a computer to the internet through a telephone or cable line

ex.en: He reset the modem to fix the internet connection.
ex.ko: 그는 인터넷 연결을 고치려고 모뎀을 재시작했다.

moderate.en: average in amount, quality, or degree; not extreme

ex.en: The restaurant serves food at moderate prices.
ex.ko: 그 식당은 적당한 가격에 음식을 판다.

ex.en: He is known as a moderate politician.
ex.ko: 그는 온건한 정치인으로 알려져 있다.

moderate.en: lead a discussion or meeting and make sure it stays fair and orderly

ex.en: She will moderate tonight's debate.
ex.ko: 그녀가 오늘 밤 토론의 사회를 볼 것이다.

moderate.en: make something less extreme or severe

ex.en: The government tried to moderate its tone after the criticism.
ex.ko: 정부는 비판 이후 어조를 누그러뜨리려 했다.

modify.en: make small changes to something in order to improve it or make it more suitable

ex.en: They modified the plan after the meeting.
ex.ko: 그들은 회의 후 계획을 수정했다.

moisture.en: small amounts of water present in the air, in a substance, or on a surface

ex.en: The plant needs enough moisture to grow.
ex.ko: 그 식물은 자라기 위해 충분한 수분이 필요하다.

molecule.en: the smallest part of a substance that has all the chemical properties of that substance

ex.en: Water molecules are made of hydrogen and oxygen.
ex.ko: 물 분자는 수소와 산소로 이루어져 있다.

monitor.en: watch and check something regularly over a period of time

ex.en: Nurses monitor the patient's heart rate closely.
ex.ko: 간호사들은 환자의 심박수를 면밀히 관찰한다.

monitor.en: a screen that shows information from a computer

ex.en: She bought a new computer monitor.
ex.ko: 그녀는 새 컴퓨터 모니터를 샀다.

monitor.en: a person who checks that rules are being followed, for example during an exam

ex.en: A monitor walked around the exam room.
ex.ko: 감독관이 시험장 안을 돌아다녔다.

monk.en: a man who lives in a religious community and devotes his life to prayer and simple living

ex.en: The monk spent hours in quiet meditation.
ex.ko: 그 수도승은 몇 시간 동안 조용히 명상했다.

monster.en: an imaginary creature that is large, ugly, and frightening

ex.en: The children were scared of the monster in the story.
ex.ko: 아이들은 이야기 속 괴물을 무서워했다.

ex.en: The hurricane grew into a monster storm.
ex.ko: 그 허리케인은 거대한 폭풍으로 커졌다.

monthly.en: happening or done once every month

ex.en: We have a monthly meeting on the first Monday.
ex.ko: 우리는 매달 첫째 월요일에 회의를 한다.

ex.en: The rent is paid monthly.
ex.ko: 월세는 매달 지불된다.

monthly.en: a magazine that is published once a month

ex.en: She subscribes to a fashion monthly.
ex.ko: 그녀는 패션 월간지를 구독한다.

monument.en: a large structure built to remember a famous person or event

ex.en: They built a monument to honor the soldiers.
ex.ko: 그들은 군인들을 기리기 위해 기념비를 세웠다.

monument.en: an old building or place that is officially protected because of its historical importance

ex.en: The castle is now a protected national monument.
ex.ko: 그 성은 이제 보호받는 국가 유적이다.

moral.en: concerned with what is right and wrong behavior

ex.en: She always tries to make moral decisions.
ex.ko: 그녀는 항상 도덕적인 결정을 내리려고 노력한다.

moral.en: a lesson about right and wrong that a story teaches

ex.en: The moral of the fable is to be honest.
ex.ko: 그 우화의 교훈은 정직해야 한다는 것이다.

moreover.en: used to add extra information to something you have already said

ex.en: The plan is expensive; moreover, it will take years to finish.
ex.ko: 그 계획은 비용이 많이 든다. 게다가 완료하는 데 몇 년이 걸릴 것이다.

mosquito.en: a small flying insect that bites people and animals to drink their blood

ex.en: A mosquito bit me on the arm.
ex.ko: 모기가 내 팔을 물었다.

moss.en: a small green plant that grows in a thick layer on rocks, trees, or damp ground

ex.en: Moss covered the old stone wall.
ex.ko: 이끼가 오래된 돌담을 덮고 있었다.

motherland.en: the country where a person was born, especially when spoken of with strong feeling

ex.en: He returned to his motherland after many years abroad.
ex.ko: 그는 오랜 해외 생활 끝에 조국으로 돌아왔다.

ex.en: The cat stayed motionless as it watched the bird.
ex.ko: 그 고양이는 새를 지켜보며 꼼짝하지 않고 있었다.

motivate.en: make someone want to do something

ex.en: Her coach motivated her to train harder.
ex.ko: 그녀의 코치는 그녀가 더 열심히 훈련하도록 동기를 부여했다.

motivation.en: a strong reason or desire to do something

ex.en: He lost his motivation to study after the exam.
ex.ko: 그는 시험 후 공부할 의욕을 잃었다.

ex.en: Police could not find a motive for the crime.
ex.ko: 경찰은 그 범죄의 동기를 찾지 못했다.

motor.en: a machine that changes energy into movement to make something work

ex.en: The washing machine's motor stopped working.
ex.ko: 세탁기의 모터가 작동을 멈췄다.

motorcycle.cue: 두 바퀴로 타는 엔진 달린 탈것
motorcycle.en: a two-wheeled vehicle with an engine

ex.en: He rides his motorcycle to work every day.
ex.ko: 그는 매일 오토바이를 타고 출근한다.

motto.en: a short phrase that expresses a rule for behavior or a strong belief

ex.en: Her motto is 'never give up'.
ex.ko: 그녀의 좌우명은 '절대 포기하지 말자'이다.

mount.en: get up onto something, such as a horse or bicycle

ex.en: She mounted the horse and rode away.
ex.ko: 그녀는 말에 올라타고 떠났다.

mount.en: fix something onto a wall or support so it stays in place

ex.en: They mounted the TV on the wall.
ex.ko: 그들은 벽에 TV를 설치했다.

mount.en: gradually increase in amount or intensity

ex.en: Pressure was mounting on the government to act.
ex.ko: 정부가 조치를 취하라는 압박이 점점 커지고 있었다.

mount.en: used before the name of a mountain

ex.en: They climbed Mount Everest last year.
ex.ko: 그들은 작년에 에베레스트산을 등반했다.

ex.en: They watched the sunrise from the mountaintop.
ex.ko: 그들은 산꼭대기에서 일출을 지켜보았다.

mourn.en: feel or show deep sadness, especially because someone has died

ex.en: The whole town mourned the loss of the beloved teacher.
ex.ko: 온 마을이 사랑받던 선생님의 죽음을 애도했다.

movement.en: the act of moving or changing position

ex.en: The sudden movement startled the cat.
ex.ko: 갑작스러운 움직임이 고양이를 놀라게 했다.

movement.en: an organized effort by a group of people to achieve a social or political goal

ex.en: She joined the environmental movement.
ex.ko: 그녀는 환경 운동에 참여했다.

mud.cue: 비 온 뒤 축축하고 부드러운 흙

ex.en: His boots were covered in mud after the hike.
ex.ko: 하이킹 후 그의 부츠는 진흙으로 뒤덮였다.

mule.en: an animal that is the offspring of a male donkey and a female horse, used for carrying loads

ex.en: The farmer used a mule to carry supplies up the mountain.
ex.ko: 농부는 물자를 산 위로 나르기 위해 노새를 이용했다.

murderer.en: a person who has killed another person illegally

ex.en: Police finally caught the murderer.
ex.ko: 경찰은 마침내 살인자를 붙잡았다.

muscle.en: a body tissue that can contract to produce movement

ex.en: He pulled a muscle while running.
ex.ko: 그는 달리다가 근육을 다쳤다.

muscle.en: power or influence, especially of a political or economic kind

ex.en: The company has enough muscle to change the industry.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 업계를 바꿀 만큼 충분한 영향력을 가지고 있다.

mustard.en: a yellow sauce with a strong, spicy taste, often eaten with meat

ex.en: He put mustard on his hot dog.
ex.ko: 그는 핫도그에 머스터드를 발랐다.

mutual.en: shared or felt equally by two or more people

ex.en: They have mutual respect for each other.
ex.ko: 그들은 서로에게 상호 존중을 가지고 있다.

myth.en: a traditional story, especially one about gods or heroes, that explains natural events or customs

ex.en: The ancient Greeks had many myths about their gods.
ex.ko: 고대 그리스인들은 그들의 신에 관한 많은 신화를 가지고 있었다.

myth.cue: 사실이 아닌데 많은 사람이 믿는 생각
myth.en: a widely believed idea that is actually false

ex.en: It's a myth that eating at night makes you gain more weight.
ex.ko: 밤에 먹으면 살이 더 찐다는 것은 잘못된 통념이다.

nail.en: the hard, flat part that covers the tip of a finger or toe

ex.en: She painted her nails red.
ex.ko: 그녀는 손톱을 빨갛게 칠했다.

nail.en: a small, thin piece of metal with a point, used to join pieces of wood together

ex.en: He hammered a nail into the wall.
ex.ko: 그는 벽에 못을 박았다.

ex.en: She nailed the poster to the board.
ex.ko: 그녀는 포스터를 게시판에 못으로 박았다.

ex.en: The baby ran around naked after his bath.
ex.ko: 그 아기는 목욕 후 벌거벗고 뛰어다녔다.

naked.en: without the help of a device such as glasses or a telescope

ex.en: You can see the comet with the naked eye.
ex.ko: 맨눈으로도 그 혜성을 볼 수 있다.

nap.en: a short sleep, usually during the day

ex.en: The baby takes a nap every afternoon.
ex.ko: 그 아기는 매일 오후 낮잠을 잔다.

nap.en: sleep for a short time, usually during the day

ex.en: He napped on the couch after lunch.
ex.ko: 그는 점심 후 소파에서 낮잠을 잤다.

narrate.en: tell a story or describe events, often for a film or book

ex.en: The documentary is narrated by a famous actor.
ex.ko: 그 다큐멘터리는 유명 배우가 내레이션을 한다.

narrative.en: a story or a description of a series of events

ex.en: The novel follows a simple narrative about two brothers.
ex.ko: 그 소설은 두 형제에 관한 단순한 이야기를 따라간다.

narrative.en: in the form of a story

ex.en: She prefers narrative writing over academic essays.
ex.ko: 그녀는 학술 에세이보다 서술형 글쓰기를 더 좋아한다.

ex.en: They walked down a narrow street.
ex.ko: 그들은 좁은 거리를 걸어 내려갔다.

narrow.en: make something smaller in range or extent

ex.en: We need to narrow down our options.
ex.ko: 우리는 선택지를 좁혀야 한다.

ex.en: He said some really nasty things about her.
ex.ko: 그는 그녀에 대해 정말 고약한 말을 했다.

nationalist.en: a person who strongly supports the independence and interests of their own nation

ex.en: The nationalist leader called for independence from the empire.
ex.ko: 그 민족주의자 지도자는 제국으로부터의 독립을 촉구했다.

ex.en: Naturally, she was upset about the news.
ex.ko: 당연히 그녀는 그 소식에 속상해했다.

naturally.en: in a way that seems normal and not forced

ex.en: Try to smile naturally for the photo.
ex.ko: 사진 찍을 때 자연스럽게 웃어 봐.

navy blue.en: having a very dark blue color

ex.en: She bought a navy blue dress.
ex.ko: 그녀는 짙은 남색 원피스를 샀다.

nearby.en: not far away; close to a place

ex.en: We stayed at a hotel nearby the airport.
ex.ko: 우리는 공항 근처의 호텔에 묵었다.

nearby.en: in a place that is not far away

ex.en: There is a good restaurant nearby.
ex.ko: 근처에 좋은 식당이 있다.

necklace.en: a piece of jewelry worn around the neck

ex.en: She wore a pearl necklace to the wedding.
ex.ko: 그녀는 결혼식에 진주 목걸이를 착용했다.

nectar.en: a sweet liquid produced by flowers that attracts bees and other insects

ex.en: Bees collect nectar from flowers to make honey.
ex.ko: 벌은 꿀을 만들기 위해 꽃에서 화밀을 모은다.

needle.en: a thin, sharp piece of metal used for sewing or for giving injections

ex.en: She threaded the needle carefully.
ex.ko: 그녀는 조심스럽게 바늘에 실을 꿰었다.

needle.en: a thin pointer on a dial that shows a measurement

ex.en: The needle on the gauge moved to zero.
ex.ko: 계기판의 바늘이 0으로 움직였다.

negotiate.en: discuss something with someone in order to reach an agreement

ex.en: The two companies negotiated a new contract.
ex.ko: 두 회사는 새 계약을 협상했다.

negotiation.en: formal discussion between people who are trying to reach an agreement

ex.en: The negotiations lasted for several hours.
ex.ko: 그 협상은 몇 시간 동안 계속되었다.

neighborhood.en: an area of a town or city where people live

ex.en: They moved to a quiet neighborhood outside the city.
ex.ko: 그들은 도시 외곽의 조용한 동네로 이사했다.

nephew.en: the son of your brother or sister

ex.en: My nephew is turning ten this year.
ex.ko: 내 조카는 올해 열 살이 된다.

nervousness.en: the feeling of being worried or anxious

ex.en: She tried to hide her nervousness before the interview.
ex.ko: 그녀는 면접 전 긴장을 감추려 했다.

ness.en: a piece of land that sticks out into the sea, often used in place names

ex.en: The lighthouse stands on a rocky ness.
ex.ko: 그 등대는 바위투성이 곶 위에 서 있다.

network.en: a group of people or things that are connected to each other

ex.en: She built a strong network of business contacts.
ex.ko: 그녀는 탄탄한 인맥 네트워크를 구축했다.

network.en: a system of connected computers that can share information

ex.en: The office computers are all connected to the same network.
ex.ko: 사무실 컴퓨터들은 모두 같은 네트워크에 연결되어 있다.

network.en: a group of television or radio stations that broadcast the same programs

ex.en: The show airs on a major TV network.
ex.ko: 그 프로그램은 주요 방송망에서 방영된다.

neutral.en: not supporting or helping either side in a disagreement or war

ex.en: Switzerland remained neutral during the war.
ex.ko: 스위스는 전쟁 중에 중립을 유지했다.

neutral.en: (of a color) not bright or strong, such as grey, beige, or white

ex.en: The room was painted in neutral colors.
ex.ko: 그 방은 은은한 색으로 칠해졌다.

nevertheless.en: despite what has just been said

ex.en: It was raining; nevertheless, they went for a walk.
ex.ko: 비가 내리고 있었지만 그럼에도 불구하고 그들은 산책을 나갔다.

ex.en: They brought their newborn baby home from the hospital.
ex.ko: 그들은 갓 태어난 아기를 병원에서 집으로 데려왔다.

newborn.cue: 태어난 지 얼마 안 된 아기
newborn.en: a baby that was born very recently

ex.en: The newborn slept peacefully in her arms.
ex.ko: 그 신생아는 그녀의 품에서 평화롭게 잠들어 있었다.

newcomer.en: a person who has recently arrived somewhere or started a new activity

ex.en: As a newcomer to the team, she felt a bit nervous.
ex.ko: 팀에 새로 온 사람으로서 그녀는 조금 긴장했다.

ex.en: They live in a newly built apartment.
ex.ko: 그들은 새로 지어진 아파트에 산다.

next door.en: in or at the house or room next to yours

ex.en: Our best friends live next door.
ex.ko: 우리의 가장 친한 친구들은 옆집에 산다.

next-door.en: living in or being the house next to yours

ex.en: Our next-door neighbor is very friendly.
ex.ko: 우리 옆집 이웃은 매우 친절하다.

nickname.en: an informal name used instead of someone's real name

ex.en: His nickname at school was 'Tiger'.
ex.ko: 학교에서 그의 별명은 '타이거'였다.

nickname.en: give someone or something a nickname

ex.en: Friends nicknamed her 'Sunny' because of her cheerful personality.
ex.ko: 친구들은 그녀의 밝은 성격 때문에 '써니'라는 별명을 붙였다.

niece.en: the daughter of your brother or sister

ex.en: My niece just started middle school.
ex.ko: 내 조카는 이제 막 중학교에 입학했다.

nightlife.en: entertainment and activities available at night, such as bars and clubs

ex.en: The city is famous for its exciting nightlife.
ex.ko: 그 도시는 활기찬 밤 문화로 유명하다.

nod.en: move your head up and down to show agreement or greeting

ex.en: She nodded to show she understood.
ex.ko: 그녀는 이해했다는 표시로 고개를 끄덕였다.

nod.en: the act of nodding your head

ex.en: He gave a quick nod and left the room.
ex.ko: 그는 짧게 고개를 끄덕이고 방을 나갔다.

noisily.en: in a way that makes a lot of noise

ex.en: The children ran noisily down the hallway.
ex.ko: 아이들은 시끄럽게 복도를 뛰어다녔다.

none.en: not any person or thing among a group

ex.en: I asked everyone, but none of them knew the answer.
ex.ko: 모두에게 물어봤지만 그들 중 아무도 답을 몰랐다.

nonetheless.en: despite what has just been said

ex.en: The task was difficult; nonetheless, she finished it on time.
ex.ko: 그 일은 어려웠지만 그럼에도 불구하고 그녀는 제시간에 끝냈다.

nonsense.en: ideas, statements, or behavior that are silly or have no sense

ex.en: Stop talking nonsense and explain what really happened.
ex.ko: 말도 안 되는 소리 그만하고 실제로 무슨 일이 있었는지 설명해.

nor.en: used after a negative statement to show that the same is true of something else

ex.en: She doesn't like coffee, nor does she like tea.
ex.ko: 그녀는 커피를 좋아하지 않고, 차도 좋아하지 않는다.

normally.en: in the usual or ordinary way

ex.en: I normally wake up at seven o'clock.
ex.ko: 나는 보통 7시에 일어난다.

northeast.en: the direction between north and east

ex.en: The storm is moving toward the northeast.
ex.ko: 그 폭풍은 북동쪽으로 이동하고 있다.

northeast.en: in, from, or facing the northeast

ex.en: They live in the northeast part of the country.
ex.ko: 그들은 그 나라의 북동쪽 지역에 산다.

northeastern.en: situated in or belonging to the northeast

ex.en: The northeastern coast gets a lot of snow in winter.
ex.ko: 북동쪽 해안은 겨울에 눈이 많이 온다.

northern.en: situated in or belonging to the north

ex.en: Winters are harsh in the northern regions.
ex.ko: 북쪽 지역은 겨울이 혹독하다.

northwest.en: the direction between north and west

ex.en: The wind is blowing from the northwest.
ex.ko: 바람이 북서쪽에서 불어오고 있다.

northwest.en: in, from, or facing the northwest

ex.en: They traveled to the northwest coast.
ex.ko: 그들은 북서쪽 해안으로 여행을 갔다.

northwestern.en: situated in or belonging to the northwest

ex.en: The northwestern part of the city is mostly farmland.
ex.ko: 그 도시의 북서쪽 지역은 대부분 농지이다.

ex.en: There was a noticeable improvement in her English.
ex.ko: 그녀의 영어 실력에 눈에 띄는 향상이 있었다.

noticeboard.en: a board on a wall where notices are displayed for people to read

ex.en: The event was announced on the school noticeboard.
ex.ko: 그 행사는 학교 게시판에 공지되었다.

notion.en: an idea or belief about something

ex.en: He had a vague notion of what she meant.
ex.ko: 그는 그녀가 무슨 뜻으로 말했는지 막연한 생각이 있었다.

ex.en: The area is notorious for traffic jams.
ex.ko: 그 지역은 교통 체증으로 악명이 높다.

ex.en: She dreams of becoming a famous novelist.
ex.ko: 그녀는 유명한 소설가가 되는 것을 꿈꾼다.

nowhere.en: not in or to any place

ex.en: My keys are nowhere to be found.
ex.ko: 내 열쇠가 아무 곳에도 없다.

nuclear.en: relating to the energy released when the nucleus of an atom is split or joined

ex.en: The country built a new nuclear power plant.
ex.ko: 그 나라는 새로운 원자력 발전소를 건설했다.

nuclear.en: relating to a family consisting only of parents and children

ex.en: The nuclear family is common in modern society.
ex.ko: 핵가족은 현대 사회에서 흔하다.

nucleus.en: the central part of a cell that contains the genetic material

ex.en: The nucleus controls the activities of the cell.
ex.ko: 핵은 세포의 활동을 조절한다.

nucleus.en: the central and most important part of something

ex.en: These three players form the nucleus of the team.
ex.ko: 이 세 선수가 팀의 핵심을 이룬다.

ex.en: She has won numerous awards for her work.
ex.ko: 그녀는 자신의 작업으로 수많은 상을 받았다.

nutrient.en: a substance that living things need to grow and stay healthy

ex.en: Vegetables are full of important nutrients.
ex.ko: 채소에는 중요한 영양소가 가득하다.

nutrition.en: the process of taking in food needed for health and growth, or the study of this

ex.en: Good nutrition is important for children's growth.
ex.ko: 좋은 영양 섭취는 아이들의 성장에 중요하다.

nutritious.en: containing substances that are good for your health

ex.en: She always tries to cook nutritious meals for her family.
ex.ko: 그녀는 항상 가족을 위해 영양가 있는 식사를 요리하려고 한다.

object.en: express disagreement or disapproval about a plan, idea, or action

ex.en: Several members objected to the new policy.
ex.ko: 몇몇 회원들은 새 정책에 반대했다.

object.en: a thing that you can see or touch

ex.en: There was a strange object on the table.
ex.ko: 탁자 위에 이상한 물건이 있었다.

object.en: the aim or purpose of an action

ex.en: The object of the game is to score more points than your opponent.
ex.ko: 그 게임의 목적은 상대보다 더 많은 점수를 얻는 것이다.

object.en: (grammar) a noun or pronoun that receives the action of a verb

ex.en: In the sentence 'She reads books,' 'books' is the object.
ex.ko: 'She reads books' 문장에서 'books'가 목적어이다.

objection.en: a statement or feeling of disagreement or disapproval

ex.en: He raised an objection to the plan during the meeting.
ex.ko: 그는 회의 중 그 계획에 이의를 제기했다.

objective.en: based on facts rather than personal feelings or opinions

ex.en: Try to give an objective opinion about the situation.
ex.ko: 그 상황에 대해 객관적인 의견을 내려고 노력해봐.

objective.en: something that you are trying to achieve

ex.en: The main objective of the project is to reduce costs.
ex.ko: 그 프로젝트의 주된 목표는 비용을 줄이는 것이다.

observation.en: the act of carefully watching or examining something

ex.en: Careful observation helped scientists understand the bird's behavior.
ex.ko: 세심한 관찰은 과학자들이 새의 행동을 이해하는 데 도움이 되었다.

observation.en: a comment based on something you have noticed

ex.en: She made an interesting observation about the results.
ex.ko: 그녀는 결과에 대해 흥미로운 소견을 말했다.

ex.en: Scientists observed the animals in their natural habitat.
ex.ko: 과학자들은 동물들을 자연 서식지에서 관찰했다.

observe.en: follow or celebrate a custom, law, or holiday

ex.en: The family observes many traditional holidays.
ex.ko: 그 가족은 많은 전통 명절을 지킨다.

obstacle.en: something that makes it difficult to do or achieve something

ex.en: Lack of money was a major obstacle to their plans.
ex.ko: 돈 부족은 그들의 계획에 큰 장애물이었다.

ex.en: She obtained a degree in engineering.
ex.ko: 그녀는 공학 학위를 취득했다.

obvious.en: easy to see or understand; clear

ex.en: It was obvious that he was lying.
ex.ko: 그가 거짓말을 하고 있다는 것은 명백했다.

obviously.en: in a way that is easy to see or understand

ex.en: Obviously, she was upset about the decision.
ex.ko: 명백히 그녀는 그 결정에 화가 나 있었다.

occasion.en: a particular time when something happens

ex.en: On this occasion, we decided to stay home.
ex.ko: 이번 경우에는 집에 있기로 했다.

occasion.en: a special or important event, such as a celebration

ex.en: Her wedding was a joyful occasion.
ex.ko: 그녀의 결혼식은 즐거운 행사였다.

occasional.en: happening sometimes but not often or regularly

ex.en: We enjoy the occasional trip to the beach.
ex.ko: 우리는 가끔 해변으로 떠나는 여행을 즐긴다.

ex.en: He occasionally visits his grandparents on weekends.
ex.ko: 그는 가끔 주말에 조부모님을 방문한다.

occupy.en: take up space, time, or a position

ex.en: The sofa occupies most of the living room.
ex.ko: 그 소파는 거실 대부분을 차지한다.

ex.en: The family has occupied this house for generations.
ex.ko: 그 가족은 여러 세대에 걸쳐 이 집에 거주해 왔다.

occupy.en: take control of a place using military force

ex.en: The army occupied the city for several months.
ex.ko: 군대는 그 도시를 몇 달 동안 점령했다.

ex.en: The accident occurred late at night.
ex.ko: 그 사고는 늦은 밤에 발생했다.

ex.en: It occurred to her that she had forgotten her keys.
ex.ko: 그녀는 문득 열쇠를 놓고 왔다는 생각이 떠올랐다.

ocean.en: a very large area of sea

ex.en: They sailed across the Atlantic Ocean.
ex.ko: 그들은 대서양을 가로질러 항해했다.

offensive.en: causing someone to feel upset or angry

ex.en: His comment was offensive to many people.
ex.ko: 그의 발언은 많은 사람들에게 불쾌감을 주었다.

offensive.en: used for or relating to attacking rather than defending

ex.en: The team switched to an offensive strategy in the second half.
ex.ko: 그 팀은 후반전에 공격적인 전략으로 바꿨다.

officially.en: in a way that is approved by an official authority

ex.en: The new law officially takes effect next month.
ex.ko: 그 새 법은 다음 달부터 공식적으로 효력을 발휘한다.

oily.en: containing or covered with oil or grease

ex.en: The pan was still oily after washing.
ex.ko: 그 팬은 씻은 후에도 여전히 기름졌다.

old-fashioned.en: not modern; belonging to a style or way of thinking from the past

ex.en: He has some old-fashioned ideas about marriage.
ex.ko: 그는 결혼에 대해 다소 구식인 생각을 가지고 있다.

oneself.en: used to refer back to the person who is the subject of the sentence, when that person is not specified

ex.en: It's important to believe in oneself.
ex.ko: 자기 자신을 믿는 것이 중요하다.

onstage.en: happening on the stage, in front of an audience

ex.en: The actors had an onstage argument that surprised everyone.
ex.ko: 배우들의 무대 위 언쟁은 모두를 놀라게 했다.

onstage.en: on the stage, in front of an audience

ex.en: The singer felt nervous just before going onstage.
ex.ko: 그 가수는 무대에 오르기 직전 긴장했다.

onto.en: to a position on the surface of something

ex.en: The cat jumped onto the table.
ex.ko: 고양이가 탁자 위로 뛰어올랐다.

opening.en: a space or gap in something

ex.en: Light came through a small opening in the curtains.
ex.ko: 커튼 사이 작은 틈으로 빛이 들어왔다.

opening.en: the beginning of an event, such as a show or a new business

ex.en: We attended the opening of the new art gallery.
ex.ko: 우리는 새 미술관 개관식에 참석했다.

opening.en: a job or position that is available

ex.en: There is a job opening in the marketing department.
ex.ko: 마케팅 부서에 채용 공석이 있다.

operation.en: a medical procedure in which a surgeon cuts into the body to treat a problem

ex.en: She had an operation on her knee last year.
ex.ko: 그녀는 작년에 무릎 수술을 받았다.

operation.en: the way something works or is run

ex.en: The new factory began operation last month.
ex.ko: 그 새 공장은 지난달 가동을 시작했다.

operation.en: a planned activity involving many people, especially a military or police action

ex.en: The police carried out a large operation against drug dealers.
ex.ko: 경찰은 마약상들을 대상으로 대규모 작전을 실시했다.

opposition.en: strong disagreement with or resistance to something

ex.en: There was strong public opposition to the new tax.
ex.ko: 새 세금에 대한 대중의 반대가 거셌다.

opposition.en: the political party that is not currently in power

ex.en: The opposition criticized the government's decision.
ex.ko: 야당은 정부의 결정을 비판했다.

oppress.en: treat a person or group unfairly by using power or force to control them

ex.en: The dictator oppressed anyone who disagreed with him.
ex.ko: 그 독재자는 자신에게 반대하는 사람은 누구든 억압했다.

oppression.en: the state of being treated unfairly through the cruel use of power or force

ex.en: The people finally rose up against years of oppression.
ex.ko: 그 사람들은 마침내 수년간의 억압에 맞서 봉기했다.

option.cue: 여러 가지 중 선택할 수 있는 것
option.en: one of the choices you can make in a situation

ex.en: We have several options for dinner tonight.
ex.ko: 오늘 저녁 식사에는 몇 가지 선택지가 있다.

ex.en: She gave an oral presentation in class.
ex.ko: 그녀는 수업에서 구두 발표를 했다.

oral.en: an exam in which you answer questions by speaking

ex.en: He passed his French oral with high marks.
ex.ko: 그는 프랑스어 구술시험에서 높은 점수를 받았다.

orbit.cue: 천체가 다른 천체 주위를 도는 길
orbit.en: the curved path that an object follows as it moves around a planet, star, or moon

ex.en: The satellite moves in orbit around the Earth.
ex.ko: 그 위성은 지구 주위를 궤도를 그리며 돈다.

orbit.en: move in a circular path around a planet, star, or moon

ex.en: The moon orbits the Earth once a month.
ex.ko: 달은 한 달에 한 번 지구 궤도를 돈다.

orchestra.en: a large group of musicians who play different instruments together

ex.en: The orchestra performed a beautiful symphony.
ex.ko: 그 오케스트라는 아름다운 교향곡을 연주했다.

orderly.en: arranged in a neat, careful, or organized way

ex.en: The students left the building in an orderly manner.
ex.ko: 학생들은 질서 있게 건물을 빠져나갔다.

ordinary.en: normal or usual, not special or different

ex.en: It was just an ordinary day at work.
ex.ko: 그날은 그냥 평범한 근무일이었다.

organ.en: a part of the body that has a particular function, such as the heart or lungs

ex.en: The heart is a vital organ.
ex.ko: 심장은 중요한 장기이다.

organ.en: a large musical instrument with a keyboard, played especially in churches

ex.en: She played the organ during the church service.
ex.ko: 그녀는 예배 중 오르간을 연주했다.

organic.en: (of food) grown without artificial chemicals such as fertilizers or pesticides

ex.en: They only buy organic vegetables.
ex.ko: 그들은 유기농 채소만 구입한다.

organic.en: relating to or coming from living things

ex.en: Fossil fuels come from organic matter.
ex.ko: 화석 연료는 유기물에서 나온다.

organism.en: a living thing, such as an animal, plant, or bacterium

ex.en: Every organism needs energy to survive.
ex.ko: 모든 생물은 생존하기 위해 에너지가 필요하다.

organization.en: a group of people who work together for a particular purpose

ex.en: She works for a charity organization.
ex.ko: 그녀는 자선 단체에서 일한다.

organization.en: the way in which the parts of something are arranged or planned

ex.en: The report lacked clear organization.
ex.ko: 그 보고서는 명확한 체계가 부족했다.

origin.en: the point or place where something begins or comes from

ex.en: The origin of the word is Latin.
ex.ko: 그 단어의 기원은 라틴어이다.

ex.en: Hurry up, otherwise we'll miss the train.
ex.ko: 서둘러, 안 그러면 기차를 놓칠 거야.

otherwise.en: apart from what has just been mentioned

ex.en: The kitchen needs cleaning, but otherwise the house is ready.
ex.ko: 부엌은 청소가 필요하지만 그 밖에는 집이 준비되었다.

ought to.en: used to say what is the right or sensible thing to do

ex.en: You ought to apologize to her.
ex.ko: 너는 그녀에게 사과해야 한다.

out-of-date.en: no longer useful, valid, or fashionable

ex.en: This map is out-of-date; the road has changed.
ex.ko: 이 지도는 오래되어서 도로가 바뀌었다.

outdoor.en: happening or used outside a building

ex.en: They enjoy outdoor activities like hiking and camping.
ex.ko: 그들은 하이킹과 캠핑 같은 야외 활동을 즐긴다.

outdoors.en: outside a building, in the open air

ex.en: The children love to play outdoors.
ex.ko: 아이들은 야외에서 노는 것을 좋아한다.

outer.en: on the outside of something, or further from the center

ex.en: The outer layer of the cake was covered in chocolate.
ex.ko: 케이크의 바깥층은 초콜릿으로 덮여 있었다.

outlaw.en: a person who has broken the law and is hiding from the authorities

ex.en: The movie is about a famous outlaw in the Old West.
ex.ko: 그 영화는 서부 개척 시대의 유명한 무법자에 관한 이야기이다.

ex.en: The country outlawed smoking in public places.
ex.ko: 그 나라는 공공장소에서의 흡연을 금지했다.

outline.en: a general description or plan of something, showing only the main points

ex.en: She gave a brief outline of the project.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 프로젝트의 간단한 개요를 설명했다.

outline.en: describe the main points of something

ex.en: The manager outlined the new plan to the team.
ex.ko: 그 매니저는 팀에게 새 계획의 개요를 설명했다.

outstanding.en: extremely good; much better than average

ex.en: She received an award for outstanding performance.
ex.ko: 그녀는 뛰어난 성과로 상을 받았다.

outward.en: relating to how something appears on the outside rather than what it truly is

ex.en: Despite his outward calm, he was terrified inside.
ex.ko: 겉으로는 침착해 보였지만 그는 속으로 겁에 질려 있었다.

outweigh.en: be greater or more important than something else

ex.en: The benefits of the plan outweigh the risks.
ex.ko: 그 계획의 이점이 위험보다 더 크다.

overcome.en: successfully deal with or defeat a problem or difficulty

ex.en: She overcame many challenges to reach her goal.
ex.ko: 그녀는 목표를 이루기 위해 많은 어려움을 극복했다.

ex.en: She was overjoyed to see her family again.
ex.ko: 그녀는 가족을 다시 만나 매우 기뻐했다.

overnight.en: during or for the length of one night

ex.en: We stayed overnight at a small hotel.
ex.ko: 우리는 작은 호텔에서 하룻밤을 묵었다.

overnight.en: lasting for or happening during the night

ex.en: They went on an overnight trip to the mountains.
ex.ko: 그들은 산으로 1박 여행을 떠났다.

overwhelm.en: have such a strong emotional effect on someone that they cannot cope

ex.en: She was overwhelmed with joy when she heard the news.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 소식을 듣고 기쁨에 압도되었다.

overwhelm.cue: 감당 못할 정도로 많은 일이 몰려오다
overwhelm.en: give someone so many tasks or problems that they cannot deal with them all

ex.en: The volunteers were overwhelmed with requests for help.
ex.ko: 자원봉사자들은 도움 요청이 너무 많아 정신이 없었다.

overwhelming.en: so great or strong that it is difficult to deal with or resist

ex.en: The team won by an overwhelming majority.
ex.ko: 그 팀은 압도적인 표차로 이겼다.

overwork.en: the act of working too much or too hard

ex.en: He collapsed from overwork.
ex.ko: 그는 과로로 쓰러졌다.

overwork.en: make someone or yourself work too hard

ex.en: She has been overworking herself lately.
ex.ko: 그녀는 최근 스스로를 혹사시키며 일하고 있다.

owe.en: have a duty to pay someone back money that they gave or lent you

ex.en: I still owe him twenty dollars.
ex.ko: 나는 아직 그에게 20달러를 빚지고 있다.

owe.en: feel grateful to someone because of something they did for you

ex.en: I owe my success to my parents' support.
ex.ko: 나는 부모님의 지원 덕분에 성공을 이루었다.

ex.en: There is a balance of $200 owing on the account.
ex.ko: 그 계좌에는 200달러의 미지급금이 있다.

owing.en: used to say what caused something, as in 'owing to'

ex.en: The flight was delayed owing to bad weather.
ex.ko: 그 비행기는 악천후 때문에 지연되었다.
